accounts.latestUpdate: Workshop for Lightning Division pending. Org transition delaying stakeholders. ALC renewal + 22X→26X upgrade and attribute creation also in scope.

sessions.notes: Workshop planned for Lightning Division. Org transition causing stakeholder unavailability; expected to stabilize by end of May. ALC renewal (lapsed since Mar 2026), 22X→26X upgrade, and attribute creation running in parallel. 60 additional users on KTIA site already under consideration. Q3 target.

accounts.latestUpdate: ~30–40 users. 3DExperience workshop to be scheduled via Sahil. German team offloading projects to India. Parallel KATYA VFi RFP looks positive for Q2.

sessions.notes: ~30–40 users; requirement started with PM but expanding to unified enterprise platform. Multiple siloed tools in use — consolidation objective. Manish and Sahil co-visited; workshop agreed. Existing SMRT/V6 managed by German team facing high attrition; India ops expanding. Parallel: KATYA VFi RFP (positive for Q2) + Kayisa VFi team requesting basic quote for PCS + MDG (Hyundai-driven). Scope too wide for Q2 — Q3 target.

accounts.latestUpdate: No customer engagement yet. Qualification stage. Update expected next cadence.

sessions.notes: No customer meeting yet. Qualification stage only. Soham Vader to provide update in next session.

accounts.latestUpdate: No customer engagement yet. Qualification stage. Update expected next cadence.

sessions.notes: No customer meeting yet. Qualification stage only. Soham Vader to provide update in next session.

accounts.latestUpdate: Existing account in hypercare. User resistance + high attrition making training ineffective. Focus is stabilization, not expansion.

sessions.notes: Existing deployment in stabilization mode. Challenges: (1) User mindset resistance; (2) High attrition — trained users keep leaving; (3) Production timelines consuming capacity meant for adoption. Prasanth to visit Chennai, meet Mr. Sehru in person; Manoj to join online.
Customer meeting planned this week to confirm go-forward engagement plan.

accounts.latestUpdate: Small deal — NetWipes base/reuse only. Customer has no PDM system. Meeting planned this week to align on demo scope and timeline.

sessions.notes: Customer not yet confirmed meeting/demo slot. Prasanth actively chasing. Q2 June target maintained.

Small deal: NetWipes base/reuse only. Customer has no PDM system. Meeting missed last week due to partner sales kickoff. Meeting planned this week — align on demo scope, requirements, and timeline. Q2 target maintained.

accounts.latestUpdate: Proposal submitted. ENOVIA+Delmia bundle strategy. DS Dir Deepak–Grindwell Dir management-level call not yet done. Q2 closure target at risk.

sessions.notes: Proposal submitted. Siemens Delmia also evaluated — DS strategy: bundle ENOVIA+Delmia in single commercial. DS Director Deepak personally close with Grindwell Director — Rashakar to connect them for management-level push. Q2 target.
No fresh update. Rashakar–Deepak call not yet done. Cloud ₹56L / On-Prem ₹76L confirmed. Prasanth to push Joe + Rashakar to expedite management call. Q2 closure target maintained.

accounts.latestUpdate: NDA received from customer; DS legal reviewing. Delmia/Robotics discussion done. ENOVIA exploratory not yet started — target mid-June. Moved to Q3.

sessions.notes: NDA not yet signed — customer side delay. ENOVIA exploratory stage not started; relevant stakeholder not yet connected. NDA is the gate before any data sharing or discovery. June target tentative.

NDA received; DS legal currently reviewing. Delmia/Robotics discussion completed during Titan site visit. ENOVIA exploratory could not start — stakeholder unavailable. Timeline moved to Q3; target to complete exploratory + initial demos by mid-June.

accounts.latestUpdate: Two demo levels completed. Management review in progress. CAD + NetWipes primary; ENOVIA foundational. Shiv to join next high-level call.

sessions.notes: Two engagement levels done: (1) Titan site visit; (2) Titan team at DS/partner office — Rajiv, Ganesh, Gopal present. End-to-end PLM showcased with AI/generative modeling. Cloud/SaaS positioned as primary. Srini Vasan (Director, Watches Division) confirmed investment readiness. Management approval awaited. Legacy data migration in scope (Umashankar). Q2-end/Sep target.

Customer mail received 28 May — solution under management review. CAD + NetWipes are primary components; ENOVIA positioning is foundational (Gopal's framing). ENOVIA portion small and still undecided. Shiv to be included in next high-level call.

accounts.latestUpdate: Meeting with design manager scheduled 27 May. Update to follow in next cadence.

sessions.notes: Meeting with design manager scheduled for 27 May. Update to be shared in next cadence call.

accounts.latestUpdate: On hold. Implementation cost shared with sales. User count not confirmed. One more customer call before pipeline removal decision.

sessions.notes: Implementation cost estimated and shared with Mohit (sales). User count still not confirmed. One more customer call to be done before deciding to keep or remove from active pipeline.

accounts.latestUpdate: Cloud deal. 2 roles for BOM management. Proposal shared. FY27 next-year timeline.

sessions.notes: Cloud deal. 2 BOM management roles. Proposal shared with customer. Timeline is next year (FY27).

accounts.latestUpdate: Cloud proposal ~₹25L submitted. BPW missing in cloud BOM. On-prem proposal submitted by sales without tech involvement — Minutus not yet engaged for effort estimate. Defense projects may influence deployment decision.

sessions.notes: Two options: on-cloud (~₹25L) and on-premise. Cloud BOM prepared with Abyss input. BPW (Business Process Worker) missing — at least 1 BPW required for 8–10 BPO users to enable dashboarding and governance. On-prem proposal submitted by Digpal (sales) without ALTEM tech team or Minutus input — flagged as red flag by Shiv. On-prem estimate: 2–3 months, 2 Minutus resources = ~₹18L. Defense project context may influence deployment choice. Next: Suraj to obtain on-prem proposal, loop in Minutus, get Abyss feedback.

accounts.latestUpdate: Initial discovery call done in Feb 2026. Full quarter gap — context reset needed. Pain: PDM + CHG via Excel/email. Shiv or Manish Jha must attend next customer meeting.

sessions.notes: Primary discovery call done Feb 2026. Full quarter elapsed — context needs reset. Pain identified: PDM (document/part management) and CHG (change management) currently via Excel and email. Must be consultative engagement — no license/demo push. Senior DS person (Shiv or Manish Jha) to attend next customer meeting. Suraj Mamidwar in Chakan region but occupied — Yash to coordinate scheduling. Customer available after 8 Jun.

accounts.latestUpdate: Urban Air Mobility startup. No PLM — ECR/ECM via Excel. Phase 1 scope: PDM + CHG + PM; 20–50 users TBC. DS campus visit 9 Jun for pain discovery. On-prem preferred (defense compliance).

sessions.notes: Urban Air Mobility startup, Chakan. Drones (defense) + quadcopter EV vision. Acquired EV car design data (CATIA V5) from bankrupt US startup — modular platform (sedan/crossover/hatchback). No revision mgmt tool; ECR/ECM via Excel and email; no PM system. Phase 1: PDM + CHG + PM; 20–50 users TBC. On-prem preferred (defense; not open to public cloud). BridgeInstar (another DS partner) proposed private cloud — Shiv advised against (user count below minimum threshold). DS campus visit Hinjawadi 9 Jun — focus on quantifying pain, not demo. Push case.

accounts.latestUpdate: On hold. Customer not available. No scope, requirements, or user count established.

sessions.notes: Initial overview done. No scope/requirements/user count. Customer not confirmed for discovery meeting. Placed on hold — will revisit when customer shows intent.

accounts.latestUpdate: Active implementation. PCS + UEFI config in progress for CATIA V5 connection. Config error encountered — awaiting CATIA V5 UEFI docs from Akash (DS).

sessions.notes: 3DEXPERIENCE 2024X deployed and in active use. Customer confirmed readiness for CATIA VFi via PCS + UEFI. Admin PCS cleared. Yash doing client-side config — hitting config error; no documentation available for this specific scenario. Action: Yash to email Akash (DS) for CATIA V5 UEFI configuration docs.

accounts.latestUpdate: Post-installation issue. 3DSpace Index down — consolidation port error. SR unresponsive 4–5 days. CRIT SIT to be raised. Escalate to Shiv if no response in 1–2 days.

sessions.notes: 3DEXPERIENCE 2026 FD02 deployed in 3 days. Post-install validation Day 2: 3DSpace Index stopped working — consolidation port error. KB search done (2 QA docs followed), SR raised, Akash consulted, SR reinstall done — issue persists. SR unresponsive 4–5 days. Customer escalating to Yash repeatedly. Action: raise CRIT SIT immediately; if no response in 1–2 days, email Shiv to loop in SR Management.

accounts.latestUpdate: Partner ownership conflict: ALTEM + Solize + Minutus all active on same activity without clear ownership. ALTEM not to proceed until ownership and payment terms resolved.

sessions.notes: SQL DB on separate server confirmed intact. Three-party conflict: customer insisting ALTEM and Solize work simultaneously; Minutus also ready on SQL DB migration. Shiv flagged this as unacceptable. Solize confirmed readiness; escalation from IAC to Solize done. Shiv: only one partner can own services delivery. ALTEM must confirm formal engagement scope and payment terms before any work. Suraj committed to not proceeding without ownership resolution.

accounts.latestUpdate: On hold. Customer expects custom Project Dashboards. Technical value proposal needed to build confidence on custom dashboard possibilities for Project Management.

sessions.notes: Not discussed this session. Background: Proposal submitted last quarter. MEPL team expecting custom Project Dashboards — technical value proposal needed to build confidence before re-engagement.

accounts.latestUpdate: BPM solution proposed. Mr. Alok confirmed confidence in solution. Budget approval expected June 1st week. No technical blockers.

sessions.notes: BPM Solutions proposed; proposal submitted last quarter. Mr. Alok (Alok Gupta) confirmed confidence in the BPM solution. Budget approval expected by June 1st week — once confirmed, deal can be concluded. No technical blockers.

accounts.latestUpdate: Budgetary proposal submitted; SBO approval pending DS side. Live Creo on-release demo scheduled 5 Jun. Critical blocker: Creo connector UEP+UDO installation pending on customer system.

sessions.notes: Proposal submitted; SBO approval pending DS side. Technical demo done — excellent feedback across the board. After Market team wants live Creo on-release demo (not recording). ~15 users in scope. Creo connector (UEP+UDO) installation on customer system is critical blocker for Thu 5 Jun demo. On-Release mode requires STS role license — Mr. Vinay confirmed release license available. Windchill 3-year subscription nearing expiry — active competitive evaluation.

accounts.latestUpdate: 4-process BPM architecture defined: CRM, RFQ Inquiry Register, RFQ Execution, APQP. BPM→DPM auto-trigger confirmed. DPM for project summary view; XPP for Director-facing dashboard. Commercial proposal after Director sign-off.

sessions.notes: POC ongoing. Scope: custom NPD PM + Sales/Marketing RFQ MIS Report. New progress: CRM process via BPM identified. 4-process BPM architecture validated: (1) CRM — sales forecast/budget; (2) RFQ Inquiry Register — Excel tracker migrated to platform; (3) RFQ Timeline/Execution; (4) APQP — template-based. BPM→DPM event trigger: Inquiry Register event auto-creates RFQ project in DPM from template. DPM selected for project summary (Shiv validated). XPP recommended for Director-facing prompt-based dashboard. Commercial proposal after Director POC sign-off.

accounts.latestUpdate: Proposal submitted. Mr. Redek returning ~10 Jun from Poland. Direct commercial proposal to be submitted on return. Customer acknowledged 3DEXPERIENCE capabilities and scalability.

sessions.notes: Proposal submitted last quarter. Mr. Redek (key stakeholder) was in Poland post-April; returning ~10 Jun. Background: prior hands-on experience with 3DEXPERIENCE (Europe) and Teamcenter — actively evaluating both. Mr. Redek acknowledged 3DEXPERIENCE has strong capabilities and good scalability. Industry: automotive add-ons (infotainment, USB chargers, vehicle accessories). Direct commercial proposal to be submitted on return.
